32.reason: [This increment causes the loop to skip the next character after '-', which may be part of another expansion or a regular character. Also, when multiple conditions match (e.g., overlapping ranges), it prints expanded characters multiple times. Additionally, accessing str[i-1] when i=0 leads to undefined behavior (reading before start of string).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop increments `i` here, but the `for` loop also increments `i` at the end of each iteration, causing it to skip the next character after processing '-'. This leads to incorrect output when multiple '-' are present or when characters follow a range.]

43.reason: [Using `scanf("%s", ...)` only reads up to the first whitespace, but the problem does not specify that input has no spaces. However, more critically, the program may access `str[i-1]` when `i=0` (i.e., if string starts with '-'), leading to out-of-bounds read.]
